narrative.headline: What 2017 Ram 2500 owners report most
narrative.summary: Brake-system warning lights activating unexpectedly — often persisting after dealer visits without a confirmed fix — represent the dominant pattern in owner complaints for this truck. Electrical faults, including a wiring fire that started while the vehicle was parked, form the second most-reported concern. A smaller share of owners also report cruise-control and throttle behavior that does not return to idle as expected after releasing the accelerator.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Owners report ABS and traction-control warning lights illuminating intermittently, with dealers unable to reproduce or resolve the fault until a diagnostic code eventually sets. · 1 fire · 6 crash · 2 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report wiring shorting out near the spare tire area, in at least one case igniting a fire while the vehicle was parked and unattended for several hours. · 1 fire

issues.title: Hydraulic
issues.blurb: Owners report simultaneous illumination of ABS, traction-control, and service anti-lock braking system warnings while driving at various speeds, sometimes accompanied by four-wheel-drive system messages. · 1 crash

issues.sample: MY RAM 2500 DIESEL WAS IN CRUISE CONTROL ON A 4 LANE HIGHWAY WITH PERIODIC STOP LIGHTS WHEN I PUSHED HEAVILY ON THE GAS PEDAL TO ACCELERATE WHILE PULLING A 13,000 LB TRAILER.  WHEN I TOOK MY FOOT OFF THE GAS, THE ENGINE STAYED IN FULL ACCELERATION MODE.  FULL BRAKING WAS NOT SLOW
issues.title: Vehicle Speed Control
issues.blurb: Owners report the engine continuing to run at elevated power after releasing the accelerator while in cruise control and towing, rather than returning to idle as expected. · 1 fire
